title: LFU Cache
problem_statement: Design and implement a Least Frequently Used (LFU) cache with get and put operations in O(1) time. When capacity is reached, evict the least frequently used key; if multiple keys have the same frequency, evict the least recently used among them.

examples.input: LFUCache cache = new LFUCache(2); cache.put(1, 1); cache.put(2, 2); cache.get(1); cache.put(3, 3); cache.get(2); cache.get(3);
examples.explanation: Key 2 is evicted because key 1 is used more frequently before inserting key 3.

constraints: At most 10^5 calls to get and put
intuition: Track each key's frequency and maintain a bucketed list of keys by frequency so the least frequently used key can be found in constant time.

approaches.name: Frequency buckets with LinkedHashSet
approaches.description: Use a map from key to value, a map from key to frequency, and a map from frequency to an ordered set of keys. Update frequency buckets on each access and evict from the lowest-frequency bucket when needed.
approaches.time: O(1) amortized for get and put
approaches.why_not: A priority queue would require O(log n) updates for every frequency change.

interview_qa.q: How do you choose the key to evict?
interview_qa.a: Evict the key with the lowest access frequency, breaking ties by removing the least recently used key in that frequency bucket.
Keeping the current minimum frequency lets you find the eviction bucket in constant time.
